title: Rhynchospora surinamensis
taxonomy: Rhynchospora surinamensis is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a type of monocotyledonous plant, which is a category of flowering plants with one seed leaf. The family Cyperaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sedges and sedge-like plants. Rhynchospora surinamensis is further classified into the tribe Rhynchosporeae, which is a subgroup of sedge plants within the Cyperaceae family. This classification helps to organize and identify the plant's characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Rhynchospora surinamensis, also known as the Surinam beaksedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rhynchospora surinamensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
